title: Blocked consideration of gun safety measures after mass shootings
description: Following the 2019 mass shootings in El Paso and Odessa, Rep. Schaefer used a procedural maneuver (point of order) to block colleagues from attaching gun safety amendments to unrelated legislation during a special session. He publicly stated he would not support any new gun restrictions and that prayer was the appropriate response.
whyItMatters: Regardless of one's position on gun policy, using procedural tactics to prevent any discussion of the issue after mass casualty events raises questions about whether all perspectives in the district are being represented. Voters should know their representative's approach to these debates.

title: Property tax elimination promise remains unrealized after a decade
description: Rep. Schaefer has campaigned since 2013 on eliminating property taxes entirely and shifting to consumption-based taxation. Despite over a decade in office, property taxes remain the primary funding mechanism for local services in HD-6, and no serious elimination proposal has advanced through the legislature.
whyItMatters: Promising to eliminate property taxes is an ambitious pledge that resonates with homeowners in Henderson, Anderson, Smith, and Cherokee counties. After ten-plus years without meaningful progress on elimination, voters should weigh this promise against the legislative reality.

title: Among most ideologically rigid members of Texas House
description: Legislative scorecards from multiple organizations consistently rank Rep. Schaefer as one of the most ideologically conservative members of the Texas House. While this aligns with many constituents' values, his unwillingness to compromise has contributed to stalled legislation on issues affecting East Texas, including rural healthcare and infrastructure funding that required bipartisan support.
whyItMatters: Ideological consistency can be a virtue, but when it prevents an official from building coalitions to pass legislation that benefits the district, East Texas voters may end up with strong rhetoric but weak results on the issues that matter most to them.

title: Voted against Medicaid expansion that would have helped rural East Texas hospitals
description: Rep. Schaefer has consistently opposed any form of Medicaid expansion in Texas, including proposals tailored to rural communities. Multiple rural hospitals in and near HD-6 have closed or face financial distress, and healthcare advocates have argued that expansion would provide a critical financial lifeline.
whyItMatters: Rural hospital closures directly affect East Texans' access to emergency care, maternity services, and routine healthcare. When a representative opposes funding mechanisms that could keep these facilities open, constituents should understand the real-world consequences.
